claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To get started, could you tell me the **name(s) of the child or children** you're enquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I'm enquiring about my child, Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So I'm assessing eligibility for **Alex**.

Before I proceed, let me first read the eligibility guidelines to ensure I have the correct criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! I've reviewed the eligibility rules. Now I have some questions to help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

**First question:** How old is Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. So Alex is 6 years old, which means they're under 16, so age-wise they could be eligible.

**Next question:** Do you live with Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, let me ask a follow-up question.

**Are you paying towards Alex's upkeep** (such as food, clothes, pocket money, or contributions towards their accommodation)? If so, are these contributions at least equal to the amount of Child Benefit you would receive?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help with things like clothes, food, and other expenses. I contribute about £30 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful.

**Next question:** How long has Alex been living with someone else (rather than with you)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has been living with someone else for quite a while now - more than a few months.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's useful context.

**Next question:** Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else made a claim for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

**Next question:** Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital, care, or residential accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just to clarify one more thing:

**Are you a UK resident, and do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's an important detail.

**One final question:** Do you have any special status that might allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad, such as being a Crown servant, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any special status like that. I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the rules based on what you've told me:

According to the eligibility criteria, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You can be responsible for a child if either:
1. You live with them, or
2. You're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them

However, there's a crucial rule: **Living abroad** - You may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. Since you don't live in the UK and don't have Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status, this affects your eligibility.

Additionally, while you do contribute £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (which would normally satisfy the contribution requirement), the fundamental requirement to claim Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK, unless you have specific exemptions (such as Crown servant status or certain other circumstances).

Since you're living abroad without the qualifying exemptions, you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

While you do meet several requirements (Alex is under 16, you're not living with Alex but are contributing significantly towards their upkeep), the key barrier is that **you do not live in the UK**. Child Benefit eligibility requires you to be a UK resident, unless you fall into specific exemptions such as being a Crown servant or having other qualifying statuses - which don't apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future—for example, if you move back to the UK—you may become eligible to claim. You could also contact HMRC directly to discuss your specific situation further, as they may be able to provide additional guidance based on any unique circumstances.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. You are contributing £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep (food, clothes, and other expenses), which meets the contribution requirement. However, you do not live in the UK and do not have any qualifying exemptions such as Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit only if you live in the UK, unless you have specific exemptions. Since none of these exemptions apply to your circumstances, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Although the claimant meets the age and contribution requirements for Child Benefit for Alex, they are not eligible because they live abroad without qualifying exemptions (such as Crown servant status). Child Benefit eligibility requires the claimant to live in the UK unless specific exemptions apply, which do not apply in this case."]
